entries.title: Storage Access
entries.description: The document.requestStorageAccess() method allows content in iframes to request storing and reading cookies and other site data, while the document.hasStorageAccess() method checks if such access is granted.

entries.title: hasStorageAccess
entries.description: The document.requestStorageAccess() method allows content in iframes to request storing and reading cookies and other site data, while the document.hasStorageAccess() method checks if such access is granted.

entries.title: localStorage
entries.description: The localStorage and sessionStorage APIs store data as key-value pairs. While localStorage persists across sessions, sessionStorage data is discarded when the page is closed.

entries.title: sessionStorage
entries.description: The localStorage and sessionStorage APIs store data as key-value pairs. While localStorage persists across sessions, sessionStorage data is discarded when the page is closed.

entries.title: IndexedDB
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBObjectStore
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBTransaction
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBIndex
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBKeyRange
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: Cache Storage
entries.description: The service worker of a website is a script that runs in its own thread and which acts as local proxy that intercepts network requests from the website. Use a service worker to implement advanced caching strategies, offline support, background tasks, or push notification support on your website.

entries.title: Storage Manager
entries.description: The navigator.storage API provides information about the availability and persistence of the data that a site stores on the device, by using APIs such as the Cache API or the IndexedDB API.

entries.title: Storage Buckets
entries.description: The navigator.storageBuckets API allows you to organize locally stored data into groups called storage buckets. Each bucket can have different settings, allowing the browser to manage and delete buckets independently rather than applying the same treatment to all.

entries.title: Cookie Store
entries.description: The CookieStore API is an asynchronous and promise-based API for managing cookies. It does not rely on document and so is available to service workers as well.

entries.title: BroadcastChannel
entries.description: The BroadcastChannel API allows you to send messages between same-origin browsing contexts, such as between the same page loaded in multiple tabs.

entries.title: File System Access
entries.description: The navigator.storage.getDirectory() method returns a FileSystemDirectoryHandle that is restricted to a specific origin and invisible to the user's actual file system for faster file-based applications, such as SQLite databases.

entries.title: Origin Private File System
entries.description: The navigator.storage.getDirectory() method returns a FileSystemDirectoryHandle that is restricted to a specific origin and invisible to the user's actual file system for faster file-based applications, such as SQLite databases.

entries.title: FileSystemFileHandle
entries.description: The navigator.storage.getDirectory() method returns a FileSystemDirectoryHandle that is restricted to a specific origin and invisible to the user's actual file system for faster file-based applications, such as SQLite databases.

entries.title: FileSystemWritableFileStream
entries.description: The showOpenFilePicker(), showDirectoryPicker(), and showSaveFilePicker() methods request access to files and directories on the user's device and returns a handle for reading and writing to them.

entries.title: OPFS Sync Access (in Workers)
entries.description: The navigator.storage.getDirectory() method returns a FileSystemDirectoryHandle that is restricted to a specific origin and invisible to the user's actual file system for faster file-based applications, such as SQLite databases.

entries.title: File
entries.description: The File API represents and reads from file data and metadata. Typically, you get files through other APIs, such as the  element or drag-and-drop events. You may also send file data to other APIs, such as creating images or drawing on canvases.

entries.title: FileReader
entries.description: The File API represents and reads from file data and metadata. Typically, you get files through other APIs, such as the  element or drag-and-drop events. You may also send file data to other APIs, such as creating images or drawing on canvases.

entries.title: Blob
entries.description: The File API represents and reads from file data and metadata. Typically, you get files through other APIs, such as the  element or drag-and-drop events. You may also send file data to other APIs, such as creating images or drawing on canvases.

entries.title: DataTransfer
entries.description: The Drag and Drop API lets users drag and drop elements and external files such as images onto web pages. Developers can customize which elements can become draggable, the type of feedback the draggable elements produce, and the droppable elements.

entries.title: FormData
entries.description: The XMLHttpRequest API makes HTTP requests. It's the predecessor to fetch(). Also known as XHR.

entries.title: ReadableStream
entries.description: The streams API creates, composes, and consumes continuously generated data.

entries.title: ReadableStreamDefaultReader
entries.description: The streams API creates, composes, and consumes continuously generated data.

entries.title: WritableStream
entries.description: The streams API creates, composes, and consumes continuously generated data.

entries.title: WritableStreamDefaultWriter
entries.description: The streams API creates, composes, and consumes continuously generated data.

entries.title: TransformStream
entries.description: The streams API creates, composes, and consumes continuously generated data.

entries.title: TextEncoder
entries.description: The TextEncoder API transforms a stream of code points into a byte stream with UTF-8 encoding, and TextDecoder does the reverse.

entries.title: TextDecoder
entries.description: The TextEncoder API transforms a stream of code points into a byte stream with UTF-8 encoding, and TextDecoder does the reverse.

entries.title: TextEncoderStream
entries.description: The TextEncoder API transforms a stream of code points into a byte stream with UTF-8 encoding, and TextDecoder does the reverse.

entries.title: TextDecoderStream
entries.description: The TextEncoder API transforms a stream of code points into a byte stream with UTF-8 encoding, and TextDecoder does the reverse.

entries.title: CompressionStream
entries.description: The CompressionStream and DecompressionStream APIs compress and decompress data using the gzip or deflate formats.

entries.title: DecompressionStream
entries.description: The CompressionStream and DecompressionStream APIs compress and decompress data using the gzip or deflate formats.

entries.title: Fetch
entries.description: The fetch() method makes asynchronous HTTP requests.

entries.title: UA Client Hints
entries.description: The Sec-CH-UA HTTP request header and the navigator.userAgentData API expose browser and platform information used to vary responses and application logic. User agent client hints are similar to user agent strings but more privacy-preserving and safer to parse.

entries.title: Headers
entries.description: The fetch() method makes asynchronous HTTP requests.

entries.title: Request
entries.description: The fetch() method makes asynchronous HTTP requests.

entries.title: Response
entries.description: The fetch() method makes asynchronous HTTP requests.

entries.title: AbortController
entries.description: The AbortController and AbortSignal APIs allow you to cancel an ongoing operation, such as a fetch() request.

entries.title: WebSocket
entries.description: The WebSocket API opens a two-way communication channel between the user's browser and a server.

entries.title: Server-Sent Events
entries.description: The EventSource API creates a connection to a server and listens to a stream of events sent by the server.

entries.title: WebTransport
entries.description: The WebTransport API transmits data between a client and a server, by using the HTTP/3 protocol.

entries.title: WebRTC
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: URL
entries.description: A URL object represents a web address, like https://example.com/, and parts of the address, such as the domain, path, and query string (as URLSearchParams).

entries.title: URLSearchParams
entries.description: A URL object represents a web address, like https://example.com/, and parts of the address, such as the domain, path, and query string (as URLSearchParams).

entries.title: URLPattern
entries.description: The URLPattern API creates patterns that can be matched against URLs or URL components.

entries.title: URL.canParse()
entries.description: The URL.canParse() static method checks whether a URL can be parsed into a valid URL object. It's an alternative to calling new URL() in a try … catch statement.

entries.title: URL.createObjectURL()
entries.description: The File API represents and reads from file data and metadata. Typically, you get files through other APIs, such as the  element or drag-and-drop events. You may also send file data to other APIs, such as creating images or drawing on canvases.

entries.title: AbortSignal.any()
entries.description: The AbortSignal.any() static method combines an iterable of abort signals into a single signal, with the abort reason taken from the first signal to abort.

entries.title: sendBeacon
entries.description: The navigator.sendBeacon() method asynchronously sends an HTTP POST request with a limited amount of data, typically for app state, events, and analytics data. The one-way beacon is guaranteed to be initiated before the browser unloads the page, but the sender does not get to handle the response.

entries.title: Network Information
entries.description: The navigator.connection API provides information about the network connection a device is using and fires events when the connection type changes.

entries.title: Web Worker
entries.description: The Worker() constructor runs a script in its own thread, which can send and receive messages with the script that created it. Also known as web workers.

entries.title: SharedWorker
entries.description: The SharedWorker() constructor runs a script in its own thread, which can send and receive messages with other scripts running at the same origin.

entries.title: Service Worker
entries.description: The service worker of a website is a script that runs in its own thread and which acts as local proxy that intercepts network requests from the website. Use a service worker to implement advanced caching strategies, offline support, background tasks, or push notification support on your website.

entries.title: Web Locks
entries.description: The navigator.locks API coordinates work with shared resources through mutually exclusive ownership of a resource's name. Also known as web locks.

entries.title: MessageChannel
entries.description: Channel messaging communicates one-to-one between browsing contexts on the same origin, such as between  elements or two documents using a shared worker.

entries.title: MessagePort
entries.description: Channel messaging communicates one-to-one between browsing contexts on the same origin, such as between  elements or two documents using a shared worker.

entries.title: Worklet
entries.description: The AudioWorklet API runs module code in a separate thread, specifically for non-blocking, low latency audio processing.

entries.title: queueMicrotask()
entries.description: The queueMicrotask() method schedules a function to run after the currently running synchronous JavaScript finishes but before returning to the event loop.

entries.title: IntersectionObserver
entries.description: The IntersectionObserver API asynchronously observes changes in the intersection of a target element with an ancestor element or with a top-level document's viewport.

entries.title: ResizeObserver
entries.description: The ResizeObserver API observes and reacts to changes in the size of DOM elements.

entries.title: MutationObserver
entries.description: The MutationObserver API watches for changes to the DOM tree and calls a callback function when DOM changes occur.

entries.title: PerformanceObserver
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: ReportingObserver
entries.description: The Reporting-Endpoints HTTP header and ReportingObserver() API send selected reports, such as Content Security Policy (CSP) violation reports or crash reports, to a nominated URL or callback function.

entries.title: Performance
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: PerformanceMark
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: PerformanceMeasure
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: PerformanceNavigationTiming
entries.description: The navigation performance entry and the PerformanceNavigationTiming API measures navigation events, such as loading time or the number of redirects.

entries.title: PerformancePaintTiming
entries.description: The paint performance entry and the PerformancePaintTiming API measures the duration of "paint" (also called "render") operations as a page loads. The API measures the time to First Paint (FP) and First Contentful Paint (FCP), common metrics for perceived loading times.

entries.title: requestIdleCallback
entries.description: The requestIdleCallback() API queues a function that runs in idle browser time, either at the end of a frame or when the user is inactive. Also known as the background tasks API.

entries.title: cancelIdleCallback
entries.description: The requestIdleCallback() API queues a function that runs in idle browser time, either at the end of a frame or when the user is inactive. Also known as the background tasks API.

entries.title: requestAnimationFrame
entries.description: The requestAnimationFrame() method schedules a function that runs before the next repaint. You can use it to animate content with JavaScript.

entries.title: cancelAnimationFrame
entries.description: The requestAnimationFrame() method schedules a function that runs before the next repaint. You can use it to animate content with JavaScript.

entries.title: performance.mark()
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: performance.measure()
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: performance.now()
entries.description: The performance global object and the PerformanceObserver API provide access to performance-related information for the current execution context.

entries.title: Notifications
entries.description: The Notification() constructor shows a message to the user, typically using the notification system of the host operating system.

entries.title: Web Share
entries.description: The navigator.share() method invokes the device's native sharing mechanism and passes text, links, files, and other content to share targets. Also known as the Web Share API.

entries.title: canShare()
entries.description: The navigator.share() method invokes the device's native sharing mechanism and passes text, links, files, and other content to share targets. Also known as the Web Share API.

entries.title: Screen Wake Lock
entries.description: The navigator.wakeLock.request("screen") API prevents the device's screen from dimming or being turned off.

entries.title: Idle Detection
entries.description: The IdleDetector API is used to notify a webpage of the user's idle, active, and locked state.

entries.title: Clipboard
entries.description: The navigator.clipboard API asynchronously reads and writes to the system clipboard.

entries.title: Vibration
entries.description: The navigator.vibrate() method makes a tactile alert, if the device is equipped with a haptic motor.

entries.title: App Badging
entries.description: The navigator.setAppBadge() method allows installed web applications to display an application badge, typically a dot or number shown alongside the application's icon on the device's home screen or application dock.

entries.title: clearAppBadge()
entries.description: The navigator.setAppBadge() method allows installed web applications to display an application badge, typically a dot or number shown alongside the application's icon on the device's home screen or application dock.

entries.title: Selection
entries.description: The Selection API controls and modifies user text selections within the page.

entries.title: Range
entries.description: The Selection API controls and modifies user text selections within the page.

entries.title: Fullscreen API
entries.description: The fullscreen API makes a specific element fill the whole screen and hides most browser UI.

entries.title: exitFullscreen()
entries.description: The fullscreen API makes a specific element fill the whole screen and hides most browser UI.

entries.title: requestFullscreen()
entries.description: The fullscreen API makes a specific element fill the whole screen and hides most browser UI.

entries.title: Pointer Lock
entries.description: Provides access to raw mouse movement by locking the target of mouse events to a single element and hiding the mouse cursor.

entries.title: requestPointerLock()
entries.description: Provides access to raw mouse movement by locking the target of mouse events to a single element and hiding the mouse cursor.

entries.title: Constructable Stylesheets
entries.description: The CSSStyleSheet constructor creates a new stylesheet which can be shared between a document and multiple shadow roots using adoptedStyleSheets.

entries.title: elementFromPoint
entries.description: The document.elementFromPoint() and document.elementsFromPoint() methods find the top-most element at a given point in the viewport, or all elements at a given point in the viewport, respectively.

entries.title: scrollIntoView
entries.description: The scrollIntoView() method scrolls an element's ancestor containers such that the element is visible to the user.

entries.title: toggleAttribute
entries.description: The DOM (Document Object Model) API represents HTML and XML documents as trees of nodes. You can use the API to inspect and modify the structure and content of a document.

entries.title: input.showPicker()
entries.description: The showPicker() method for  elements shows the user interface for picking a value. For example, for  it shows the interface for picking a date.

entries.title: checkValidity()
entries.description: Methods that validate form controls before submission, such as checkValidity(), reportValidity() and setCustomValidity().

entries.title: form.requestSubmit()
entries.description: The  element contains interactive controls for submitting information.

entries.title: img.decode()
entries.description: The  element adds an image into the document.

entries.title: img loading="lazy"
entries.description: The loading="lazy" attribute for  and  elements blocks loading the external resource until the user scrolls to that element's part of the page.

entries.title: dialog.showModal()
entries.description: The  HTML element represents a modal or non-modal dialog box, such as a confirmation prompt or a subwindow used to enter data.

entries.title: element.dataset
entries.description: The dataset API attaches and accesses custom data on elements, using either the dataset property of HTMLElement or attributes prefixed with data-.

entries.title: elementsFromPoint
entries.description: The document.elementFromPoint() and document.elementsFromPoint() methods find the top-most element at a given point in the viewport, or all elements at a given point in the viewport, respectively.

entries.title: exitPictureInPicture
entries.description: The picture-in-picture API allow websites to create a floating, always-on-top video window. Also known as PiP or pop-out video.

entries.title: inert attribute
entries.description: The inert HTML attribute marks an element and its descendants as non-interactive. Inert elements don't get focus or fire click events.

entries.title: matchMedia
entries.description: The window.matchMedia() method checks whether a media query applies to the document.

entries.title: <details>
entries.description: The  element is a disclosure widget which can be expanded to reveal additional content. When closed, only the nested  element is visible.

entries.title: PaymentRequest
entries.description: The PaymentRequest API prompts the user to make a payment through the browser's user interface.

entries.title: DOMParser
entries.description: The DOMParser API parses XML or HTML source code from a string into a DOM Document.

entries.title: XMLSerializer
entries.description: The XMLSerializer API provides the serializeToString() method to construct an XML string representing a DOM tree.

entries.title: NodeIterator
entries.description: The DOM (Document Object Model) API represents HTML and XML documents as trees of nodes. You can use the API to inspect and modify the structure and content of a document.

entries.title: TreeWalker
entries.description: The DOM (Document Object Model) API represents HTML and XML documents as trees of nodes. You can use the API to inspect and modify the structure and content of a document.

entries.title: PointerEvent
entries.description: Pointer events, such as pointerdown, and the PointerEvent API, represent general pointing inputs, from a wide range of devices, such as a mouse, pen or stylus, or touch with one or more fingers. Not to be confused with the pointer-events CSS property.

entries.title: InputEvent
entries.description: The input event fires when a form control changes or an element with the contenteditable attribute changes.

entries.title: SubmitEvent
entries.description: The  element contains interactive controls for submitting information.

entries.title: HashChangeEvent
entries.description: The hashchange event fires when the URL fragment identifier (the part of the URL starting with #) of the current page has changed.

entries.title: PopStateEvent
entries.description: The window.history API manipulates the browser session history, from navigations to state management, in the tab or frame that the current page is loaded in.

entries.title: StorageEvent
entries.description: The localStorage and sessionStorage APIs store data as key-value pairs. While localStorage persists across sessions, sessionStorage data is discarded when the page is closed.

entries.title: PageTransitionEvent
entries.description: The pageshow and pagehide transition events fire when a document loads or unloads due to a navigation, such as clicking on a link on a page or the back button in a browser.

entries.title: BeforeUnloadEvent
entries.description: The beforeunload event is fired when the current window is about to be unloaded. Typically this is used to display a dialog to confirm if users really want to leave the page when there is unsaved data that would be lost.

entries.title: Custom Elements
entries.description: Autonomous custom elements are HTML elements with a hyphenated tag name (like ) that have behaviors you define.

entries.title: Shadow DOM
entries.description: Shadow DOM allows you to attach encapsulated "shadow" DOM trees to elements. A shadow DOM tree is a separate component, isolated from the scripts and styles in other parts of the document. This is a part of Web Components.

entries.title: <template>
entries.description: The  HTML element holds HTML fragments which you can clone and insert into the document using JavaScript.

entries.title: <slot>
entries.description: The  HTML element is a placeholder inside a web component where consumers of the component can insert their own markup.

entries.title: ElementInternals
entries.description: Custom elements may act like built-in form elements, via the attachInternals() method of HTMLElement and the ElementInternals API.

entries.title: Constructable CSSStyleSheet
entries.description: The CSS object model API reads, creates, and modifies CSS stylesheets and inline styles. Also known as CSSOM.

entries.title: Element.attachShadow()
entries.description: Shadow DOM allows you to attach encapsulated "shadow" DOM trees to elements. A shadow DOM tree is a separate component, isolated from the scripts and styles in other parts of the document. This is a part of Web Components.

entries.title: getUserMedia
entries.description: The navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() API requests access to devices that produce audio or video streams, such as microphones or video cameras.

entries.title: Screen Capture
entries.description: The navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia() method asks the user to choose a screen or portion of a screen (such as a window) to capture as a media stream.

entries.title: MediaRecorder
entries.description: The MediaRecorder API captures data generated by MediaStream or HTMLMediaElement objects for analysis, processing, or saving to disk. Also known as the Media Recording API.

entries.title: Picture-in-Picture
entries.description: The picture-in-picture API allow websites to create a floating, always-on-top video window. Also known as PiP or pop-out video.

entries.title: MediaStream
entries.description: The navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() API requests access to devices that produce audio or video streams, such as microphones or video cameras.

entries.title: MediaStreamTrack
entries.description: The navigator.mediaDevices.getUserMedia() API requests access to devices that produce audio or video streams, such as microphones or video cameras.

entries.title: HTMLMediaElement
entries.description: The  element plays videos or movies, optionally with controls provided by the browser.

entries.title: media.canPlayType()
entries.description: The  element plays videos or movies, optionally with controls provided by the browser.

entries.title: MediaCapabilities
entries.description: The navigator.mediaCapabilities API queries the encoding and decoding abilities of the device, such as supported codecs, resolutions, and bitrates.

entries.title: MediaSource
entries.description: The MediaSource API is a custom data source for media elements commonly used for adaptive streaming. Also known as Media Source Extensions (MSE).

entries.title: SourceBuffer
entries.description: The MediaSource API is a custom data source for media elements commonly used for adaptive streaming. Also known as Media Source Extensions (MSE).

entries.title: Web Audio
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: AudioWorkletNode
entries.description: The AudioWorklet API runs module code in a separate thread, specifically for non-blocking, low latency audio processing.

entries.title: AnalyserNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: OscillatorNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: GainNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: BiquadFilterNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: ConvolverNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: DelayNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: DynamicsCompressorNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: Web MIDI
entries.description: The Web MIDI API enables selecting MIDI input and output devices and sending and receiving MIDI messages.

entries.title: Speech Synthesis
entries.description: The SpeechSynthesis API converts text to speech with artificial voices.

entries.title: SpeechSynthesisUtterance
entries.description: The SpeechSynthesis API converts text to speech with artificial voices.

entries.title: Speech Recognition
entries.description: The SpeechRecognition API converts audio into text using the device's speech recognition service.

entries.title: MediaSession
entries.description: The navigator.mediaSession API integrates with platform UI for media playback. It can be used to set metadata such as title and artwork, and to handle user actions like playing, pausing, or seeking.

entries.title: HTMLVideoElement
entries.description: The  element plays videos or movies, optionally with controls provided by the browser.

entries.title: WebCodecs: VideoFrame
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: VideoDecoder
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: VideoEncoder
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: WebCodecs: AudioData
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: AudioDecoder
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: AudioEncoder
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: WebCodecs: ImageDecoder
entries.description: The WebCodecs API provides low-level access to individual video frames and chunks of audio samples, for full control over the way media is processed.

entries.title: Canvas 2D
entries.description: The CanvasRenderingContext2D API is the "2d" rendering context for the  element. It represents a flat, two-dimensional space for drawing graphics and animations.

entries.title: Path2D
entries.description: The CanvasRenderingContext2D API is the "2d" rendering context for the  element. It represents a flat, two-dimensional space for drawing graphics and animations.

entries.title: ImageBitmap
entries.description: The createImageBitmap() global method creates an ImageBitmap object from a source such as an image, SVG, blob, or canvas. An ImageBitmap object represents pixel data that can be drawn to a canvas with lower latency than other types, such as ImageData.

entries.title: OffscreenCanvasRenderingContext2D
entries.description: The OffscreenCanvas API provides a canvas that can be drawn to off screen, with no dependencies on the DOM, which can be used to run heavy rendering operations inside a worker context.

entries.title: WebGL
entries.description: The WebGLRenderingContext API is the "webgl" rendering context for the  element. It represents a space for drawing two- and three-dimensional graphics and animations.

entries.title: WebGL2
entries.description: The WebGL2RenderingContext API is the "webgl2" rendering context for the  element. It represents a space for drawing two- and three-dimensional graphics and animations. It corresponds to OpenGL ES 3.0.

entries.title: WebGPU
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPUDevice
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPUBuffer
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPUTexture
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPUShaderModule
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPURenderPipeline
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: GPUCommandEncoder
entries.description: The navigator.gpu API performs operations such as rendering and computation on dedicated graphics hardware (also known as a Graphics Processing Unit).

entries.title: OffscreenCanvas
entries.description: The OffscreenCanvas API provides a canvas that can be drawn to off screen, with no dependencies on the DOM, which can be used to run heavy rendering operations inside a worker context.

entries.title: Web Animations
entries.description: The web animation API programmatically creates, inspects, and controls element animations, to change and synchronize appearance over time.

entries.title: KeyframeEffect
entries.description: The web animation API programmatically creates, inspects, and controls element animations, to change and synchronize appearance over time.

entries.title: Scroll Timeline
entries.description: The animation-timeline, scroll-timeline, and view-timeline CSS properties advance animations based on the user's scroll position.

entries.title: View Timeline
entries.description: The animation-timeline, scroll-timeline, and view-timeline CSS properties advance animations based on the user's scroll position.

entries.title: DOMRect
entries.description: The DOMMatrix, DOMPoint, DOMQuad and DOMRect APIs represent points, rectangles, quadrilaterals and transformation matrices within JavaScript. They can be used in transformations in CSS, , and SVG.

entries.title: DOMMatrix
entries.description: The DOMMatrix, DOMPoint, DOMQuad and DOMRect APIs represent points, rectangles, quadrilaterals and transformation matrices within JavaScript. They can be used in transformations in CSS, , and SVG.

entries.title: DOMPoint
entries.description: The DOMMatrix, DOMPoint, DOMQuad and DOMRect APIs represent points, rectangles, quadrilaterals and transformation matrices within JavaScript. They can be used in transformations in CSS, , and SVG.

entries.title: DOMQuad
entries.description: The DOMMatrix, DOMPoint, DOMQuad and DOMRect APIs represent points, rectangles, quadrilaterals and transformation matrices within JavaScript. They can be used in transformations in CSS, , and SVG.

entries.title: Geolocation
entries.description: The navigator.geolocation API requests the user's latitude, longitude, heading, and speed.

entries.title: Device Orientation
entries.description: The DeviceMotion and DeviceOrientation events report the movement and orientation of the browser's device in physical space. Note that coordinates can differ noticeably between platforms and devices.

entries.title: Battery Status
entries.description: The navigator.getBattery() API obtains information about the device's battery, including charge level and status. Also known as the Battery API.

entries.title: Ambient Light
entries.description: The AmbientLightSensor API returns the current light level in lux of the ambient light level around the device.

entries.title: Compute Pressure
entries.description: The PressureObserver API monitors CPU load, allowing you to adjust workloads in response to available computing resources. Also known as the Compute Pressure API.

entries.title: Gamepad
entries.description: The navigator.getGamepads() API accesses and responds to signals from game controllers.

entries.title: Accelerometer
entries.description: The Accelerometer, LinearAccelerationSensor and GravitySensor APIs read the acceleration applied to a device in three dimensions, either including the effect of gravity, without its effect, or only its effect, respectively.

entries.title: Gyroscope
entries.description: The Gyroscope API reads the angular velocity of a device in three dimensions.

entries.title: Magnetometer
entries.description: The Magnetometer API reads magnetic field sensor data from the device's magnetometer.

entries.title: LinearAccelerationSensor
entries.description: The Accelerometer, LinearAccelerationSensor and GravitySensor APIs read the acceleration applied to a device in three dimensions, either including the effect of gravity, without its effect, or only its effect, respectively.

entries.title: RelativeOrientationSensor
entries.description: The AbsoluteOrientationSensor and RelativeOrientationSensor APIs describe the physical orientation of a device in three-dimensional space, either in relation to the Earth's coordinate system or in relation to the device's own orientation, respectively.

entries.title: WebUSB
entries.description: The WebUSB API exposes USB compatible devices to web pages.

entries.title: WebHID
entries.description: The WebHID API provides access to Human Interface Devices (HID) that are connected to the user's device.

entries.title: Web Serial
entries.description: The navigator.serial API communicates with devices over serial ports, such as microcontrollers.

entries.title: Web Bluetooth
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: Web NFC
entries.description: The NDEFReader API reads and writes messages to near-field communication (NFC) tags.

entries.title: Presentation API
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: WebAuthn
entries.description: The web authentication API allows you to create public key-based credentials and use them for authentication, such as when signing in. Also known as WebAuthn.

entries.title: Credential Management
entries.description: The navigator.credentials API provides generic storage for authentication credentials. Other credential APIs represent specific kinds of credentials, such as password or public key credentials, that the credential management API stores.

entries.title: Web Crypto
entries.description: The Web Cryptography API performs low-level cryptographic operations, such as encryption, decryption, and signature verification. Also known as the Web Crypto API.

entries.title: SubtleCrypto
entries.description: The Web Cryptography API performs low-level cryptographic operations, such as encryption, decryption, and signature verification. Also known as the Web Crypto API.

entries.title: crypto.randomUUID()
entries.description: The Web Cryptography API performs low-level cryptographic operations, such as encryption, decryption, and signature verification. Also known as the Web Crypto API.

entries.title: Permissions
entries.description: The navigator.permissions API checks whether a permission, such as access to geolocation data, has been granted.

entries.title: WebOTP
entries.description: The OTPCredential API represents a one-time-password (OTP).

entries.title: FedCM
entries.description: The IdentityCredential API delegates authentication to a third-party identity provider, instead of using third-party cookies. Also known as FedCM.

entries.title: View Transitions
entries.description: View transitions allow you to create animated visual transitions between different states of a document.

entries.title: Popover
entries.description: The popover HTML attribute creates an overlay to display content on top of other page content. Popovers can be shown declaratively using HTML, or using the showPopover() method.

entries.title: CSS.supports()
entries.description: The CSS.supports() static method returns whether the browser supports a CSS declaration (given by two arguments, a property and value) or an @supports at-rule condition string.

entries.title: <dialog>
entries.description: The  HTML element represents a modal or non-modal dialog box, such as a confirmation prompt or a subwindow used to enter data.

entries.title: AbortSignal.timeout()
entries.description: The AbortSignal.timeout() static method returns an abort signal that automatically aborts after a specified duration.

entries.title: Scheduler API
entries.description: The scheduler API provides a way to prioritize all tasks belonging to an application.

entries.title: Navigation API
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: CSS Cascade Layers
entries.description: The @layer CSS at-rule avoids specificity conflicts by providing priority levels for different groups of CSS rules, such as low-priority styles like resets, and high-priority styles like UI components.

entries.title: CSS.registerProperty()
entries.description: The CSS.registerProperty() static method and the @property CSS at-rule register custom properties for which types and behaviors can be defined.

entries.title: attachInternals()
entries.description: Custom elements may act like built-in form elements, via the attachInternals() method of HTMLElement and the ElementInternals API.

entries.title: Element.checkVisibility()
entries.description: The checkVisibility() method checks if an element is potentially visible, with optional parameters for the kinds of visibility to check. For example, it checks whether the element has the style display: none, but can also check for visibility: hidden.

entries.title: structuredClone()
entries.description: The structuredClone() global method creates a deep copy of an object. Values that cannot be cloned can instead be transferred, making the original value no longer usable.

entries.title: CSS Container Queries
entries.description: Container size queries with the @container at-rule apply styles to an element based on the dimensions of its container.

entries.title: CSSCounterStyleRule
entries.description: The @counter-style CSS at-rule sets custom counter styles for list items. For example, you can use a sequence of specific symbols instead of numbers for an ordered list.

entries.title: CSS Custom Highlight
entries.description: Custom highlights style arbitrary text ranges, without adding extra elements to the DOM.

entries.title: CSS.highlights
entries.description: Custom highlights style arbitrary text ranges, without adding extra elements to the DOM.

entries.title: requestStorageAccessFor()
entries.description: Related website sets declares relationships between sites so the browser allows cookie and storage access between them through methods such as document.requestStorageAccessFor().

entries.title: CSS.escape()
entries.description: The CSS.escape() static method escapes a string so that it can be used in a valid CSS selector.

entries.title: CSS Houdini Paint
entries.description: The paint() CSS function creates a custom image, drawn using a paint worklet, for an element's background or border.

entries.title: WebXR
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRSession
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: atob() base64 decode
entries.description: The atob() and btoa() global functions encode and decode strings to and from base 64, respectively.

entries.title: btoa() base64 encode
entries.description: The atob() and btoa() global functions encode and decode strings to and from base 64, respectively.

entries.title: LanguageModel (Prompt API)
entries.description: The LanguageModel API prompts an on-device language model. Also known as the Prompt API.

entries.title: LanguageDetector
entries.description: The LanguageDetector API identifies the likely natural language that some text is written in. You can use this API to supplement machine translation when the source source language is not known.

entries.title: Translator
entries.description: The Translator API translates some text from one natural language to another, using machine translation.

entries.title: Summarizer
entries.description: The Summarizer API uses an on-device language model to summarize text.

entries.title: EyeDropper
entries.description: The EyeDropper API opens an eyedropper tool, a color picker that allows users to select a color from their screen.

entries.title: Shape Detection: BarcodeDetector
entries.description: The BarcodeDetector API recognizes various barcode formats in images.

entries.title: CloseWatcher
entries.description: The CloseWatcher API listens and responds to close requests on in-page components, such as modals, popovers, and sidebars.

entries.title: EditContext
entries.description: The EditContext API allows you to build rich text editors that support advanced text input, such as Input Method Editor (IME) composition, an emoji picker, or other platform-specific editing UI.

entries.title: ViewTransition
entries.description: View transitions allow you to create animated visual transitions between different states of a document.

entries.title: Local Font Access
entries.description: The window.queryLocalFonts() method returns an array of locally-installed fonts, each represented by a FontData object.

entries.title: TaskController
entries.description: The scheduler API provides a way to prioritize all tasks belonging to an application.

entries.title: TaskSignal
entries.description: The scheduler API provides a way to prioritize all tasks belonging to an application.

entries.title: Explicit Resource Management
entries.description: The using and await using declarations and the dispose and asyncDispose symbols manage the lifecycle of resources such as file handles and streams. The DisposableStack and AsyncDisposableStack objects can group, dispose, and coordinate dependencies between multiple disposable resources.

entries.title: AsyncDisposableStack
entries.description: The using and await using declarations and the dispose and asyncDispose symbols manage the lifecycle of resources such as file handles and streams. The DisposableStack and AsyncDisposableStack objects can group, dispose, and coordinate dependencies between multiple disposable resources.

entries.title: Temporal (TC39)
entries.description: The Temporal API allows you to work with dates, times, time zones, and durations. It is more powerful than the Date API.

entries.title: Observable (TC39)
entries.description: The when() method on a event target returns an Observable object, which provides a declarative API for subscribing to and operating on events. It's an alternative to addEventListener() callbacks.

entries.title: Document Picture-in-Picture
entries.description: The document picture-in-picture API creates an always-on-top window from arbitrary HTML content.

entries.title: File Handling (LaunchQueue)
entries.description: The launch_handler web app manifest member, with the client_mode property, configure how web app launches behave when an instance of the app is already open. For example, you can choose to focus the existing app instance instead of launching a new one.

entries.title: LaunchParams
entries.description: The launch_handler web app manifest member, with the client_mode property, configure how web app launches behave when an instance of the app is already open. For example, you can choose to focus the existing app instance instead of launching a new one.

entries.title: Window Controls Overlay
entries.description: The display_override: ["window-controls-overlay"] web application manifest member shows content in the title bar area of a progressive web app that is installed on a desktop device.

entries.title: VirtualKeyboard
entries.description: The navigator.virtualKeyboard API inspects and controls on-screen virtual keyboards. You can use it to programmatically hide or show a virtual keyboard or respond to the keyboard disappearing or appearing.

entries.title: HTML Sanitizer
entries.description: The Document.parseHTML() static method and the setHTML() method of Element and ShadowRoot objects parse and insert HTML into the DOM in a way that can prevent cross-site scripting attacks. The Sanitizer API can customize the sanitization process.

entries.title: Trusted Types: TrustedHTML
entries.description: Trusted types allow you to lock down insecure parts of the DOM API and prevent client-side cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

entries.title: TrustedTypePolicy
entries.description: Trusted types allow you to lock down insecure parts of the DOM API and prevent client-side cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

entries.title: trustedTypes
entries.description: Trusted types allow you to lock down insecure parts of the DOM API and prevent client-side cross-site scripting (XSS) attacks.

entries.title: Digital Credentials
entries.description: The digital credentials API issues and requests digital credentials, such as driver's licenses or ID cards, with the browser or operating system. Digital credentials extend the navigator.credentials credential management API.

entries.title: JS Self-Profiling
entries.description: The Profiler API records data about the execution of a page, which you can send to a server for later analysis. Also known as self-profiling.

entries.title: LCP (Core Web Vital)
entries.description: The largest-contentful-paint performance entry and the LargestContentfulPaint API measures  the time it takes for the largest image or text to appear. Largest contentful paint (LCP) is a common metric for perceived loading times.

entries.title: CLS (Core Web Vital)
entries.description: The layout-shift performance entry and LayoutShift API measures the layout stability of web pages based on movements of the elements on the page. They're used to calculate Cumulative Layout Shift (CLS), a common metric for perceived stability.

entries.title: RTCDataChannel
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCSessionDescription
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCIceCandidate
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCRtpReceiver
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCRtpSender
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCRtpTransceiver
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCStatsReport
entries.description: The RTCPeerConnection.getStats(), RTCRtpSender.getStats(), and RTCRtpReceiver.getStats() methods return detailed information about the status, performance, network, and media for a given WebRTC connection.

entries.title: RTCDtlsTransport
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCIceTransport
entries.description: The WebRTC API establishes real-time communication channels directly between browsers. It is commonly used in video conferencing applications.

entries.title: RTCSctpTransport
entries.description: The sctp object on RTCPeerConnection represents the negotiated SCTP transport. SCTP (Stream Control Transmission Protocol) is the protocol that RTCDataChannel uses.

entries.title: RTCEncodedAudioFrame
entries.description: The WebRTC encoded transform API allows you to modify audio and video streams in WebRTC connections. For example, it can be used for visual effects or custom codecs.

entries.title: RTCEncodedVideoFrame
entries.description: The WebRTC encoded transform API allows you to modify audio and video streams in WebRTC connections. For example, it can be used for visual effects or custom codecs.

entries.title: OfflineAudioContext
entries.description: The OfflineAudioContext API represents an audio processing graph that writes to an AudioBuffer instead of an output device.

entries.title: MediaElementAudioSourceNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: MediaStreamAudioSourceNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: MediaStreamAudioDestinationNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: MediaStreamTrackAudioSourceNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: PannerNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: IIRFilterNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: StereoPannerNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: ConstantSourceNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: ChannelMergerNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: ChannelSplitterNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: WaveShaperNode
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: PeriodicWave
entries.description: The Web Audio API creates graphs of audio nodes that handle sound inputs, synthesize sounds, apply effects, create visualizations, and output to audio devices.

entries.title: XRReferenceSpace
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRFrame
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRInputSource
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRRigidTransform
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRHitTestSource
entries.description: The getHitTestResults() method of an XRFrame object helps place objects in an augmented reality session by finding where cast rays intersect with real-world geometry.

entries.title: XRWebGLLayer
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRAnchor
entries.description: An XRAnchor object keeps track of a pose that is fixed relative to the real world. Anchors build an illusion that the placed objects are really present in the user's environment.

entries.title: XRLightProbe
entries.description: The XRLightProbe and XRLightEstimate objects represent real-world environmental lighting conditions during a WebXR session.

entries.title: XRRay
entries.description: The getHitTestResults() method of an XRFrame object helps place objects in an augmented reality session by finding where cast rays intersect with real-world geometry.

entries.title: XRPose
entries.description: The navigator.xr API represents the browser's virtual reality or augmented reality system. You can use to request a WebXR session.

entries.title: Credential
entries.description: The navigator.credentials API provides generic storage for authentication credentials. Other credential APIs represent specific kinds of credentials, such as password or public key credentials, that the credential management API stores.

entries.title: AuthenticatorAssertionResponse
entries.description: The web authentication API allows you to create public key-based credentials and use them for authentication, such as when signing in. Also known as WebAuthn.

entries.title: AuthenticatorAttestationResponse
entries.description: The web authentication API allows you to create public key-based credentials and use them for authentication, such as when signing in. Also known as WebAuthn.

entries.title: AuthenticatorResponse
entries.description: The web authentication API allows you to create public key-based credentials and use them for authentication, such as when signing in. Also known as WebAuthn.

entries.title: PasswordCredential
entries.description: The PasswordCredential API represents a username and password.

entries.title: FederatedCredential
entries.description: The FederatedCredential API represents the details required to authenticate a user using a third-party identity provider. Not to be confused with federated credential management (FedCM).

entries.title: BluetoothDevice
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: BluetoothRemoteGATTServer
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: BluetoothRemoteGATTService
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: BluetoothRemoteGATTCharacteristic
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: BluetoothUUID
entries.description: The Web Bluetooth API enables selecting and communicating with nearby Bluetooth devices.

entries.title: USBDevice
entries.description: The WebUSB API exposes USB compatible devices to web pages.

entries.title: USBConfiguration
entries.description: The WebUSB API exposes USB compatible devices to web pages.

entries.title: USBInterface
entries.description: The WebUSB API exposes USB compatible devices to web pages.

entries.title: MIDIInput
entries.description: The Web MIDI API enables selecting MIDI input and output devices and sending and receiving MIDI messages.

entries.title: MIDIOutput
entries.description: The Web MIDI API enables selecting MIDI input and output devices and sending and receiving MIDI messages.

entries.title: MIDIInputMap
entries.description: The Web MIDI API enables selecting MIDI input and output devices and sending and receiving MIDI messages.

entries.title: MIDIOutputMap
entries.description: The Web MIDI API enables selecting MIDI input and output devices and sending and receiving MIDI messages.

entries.title: IDBCursor
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBCursorWithValue
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBFactory
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBRequest
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: IDBOpenDBRequest
entries.description: The IndexedDB API is a local storage transactional object database.

entries.title: Encrypted Media (EME)
entries.description: The mediaKeys property of HTMLMediaElement and the navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess() method control the playback of content subject to digital rights management. Also known as EME.

entries.title: MediaKeySession
entries.description: The mediaKeys property of HTMLMediaElement and the navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess() method control the playback of content subject to digital rights management. Also known as EME.

entries.title: MediaKeySystemAccess
entries.description: The mediaKeys property of HTMLMediaElement and the navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess() method control the playback of content subject to digital rights management. Also known as EME.

entries.title: MediaKeyStatusMap
entries.description: The mediaKeys property of HTMLMediaElement and the navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess() method control the playback of content subject to digital rights management. Also known as EME.

entries.title: requestMediaKeySystemAccess()
entries.description: The mediaKeys property of HTMLMediaElement and the navigator.requestMediaKeySystemAccess() method control the playback of content subject to digital rights management. Also known as EME.

entries.title: MediaStreamTrackProcessor
entries.description: The MediaStreamTrackProcessor and MediaStreamTrackGenerator APIs modify the contents of a media track for merging streams, applying real-time effects like funny hats, and content overlays.

entries.title: CaptureController
entries.description: The navigator.mediaDevices.getDisplayMedia() method asks the user to choose a screen or portion of a screen (such as a window) to capture as a media stream.

entries.title: Presentation API
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: PresentationAvailability
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: PresentationConnection
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: PresentationConnectionList
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: PresentationReceiver
entries.description: The Presentation API shows or controls content on another display, such as a network-connected TV or projector.

entries.title: RemotePlayback
entries.description: The Remote Playback API initiates and controls playback of media on connected remote devices, such as smart TVs with AirPlay or Chromecast capabilities.

entries.title: PushManager
entries.description: The Push API subscribes to and receives server-initiated messages. Subscribers receive pushed messages in the background, even after periods inactive or offline.

entries.title: PushSubscription
entries.description: The Push API subscribes to and receives server-initiated messages. Subscribers receive pushed messages in the background, even after periods inactive or offline.

entries.title: PushSubscriptionOptions
entries.description: The Push API subscribes to and receives server-initiated messages. Subscribers receive pushed messages in the background, even after periods inactive or offline.

entries.title: BackgroundFetchManager
entries.description: Background fetch downloads data in the background even when the web page is closed.

entries.title: BackgroundFetchRegistration
entries.description: Background fetch downloads data in the background even when the web page is closed.

entries.title: BackgroundFetchRecord
entries.description: Background fetch downloads data in the background even when the web page is closed.

entries.title: PeriodicSyncManager
entries.description: The periodic background synchronization API fires a recurring periodicsync event in a service worker. You can use this to update data in the background at regular intervals.

entries.title: Background Sync
entries.description: The background synchronization API registers a service worker callback to run only when the device is no longer offline. You can use this to let users continue using your app while offline and synchronize with a server after reconnecting.

entries.title: NavigationHistoryEntry
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: NavigationActivation
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: NavigationDestination
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: NavigationTransition
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: NavigationPreloadManager
entries.description: The service worker of a website is a script that runs in its own thread and which acts as local proxy that intercepts network requests from the website. Use a service worker to implement advanced caching strategies, offline support, background tasks, or push notification support on your website.

entries.title: CSS Typed OM
entries.description: The CSSStyleValue and its subclasses represent CSS values as distinct types instead of only strings. Also known as typed OM.

entries.title: StylePropertyMapReadOnly
entries.description: The CSSStyleValue and its subclasses represent CSS values as distinct types instead of only strings. Also known as typed OM.

entries.title: AbsoluteOrientationSensor
entries.description: The AbsoluteOrientationSensor and RelativeOrientationSensor APIs describe the physical orientation of a device in three-dimensional space, either in relation to the Earth's coordinate system or in relation to the device's own orientation, respectively.

entries.title: GravitySensor
entries.description: The Accelerometer, LinearAccelerationSensor and GravitySensor APIs read the acceleration applied to a device in three dimensions, either including the effect of gravity, without its effect, or only its effect, respectively.

entries.title: Device Posture
entries.description: The device posture API and the device-posture CSS media feature reflect the physical posture of a device, such as whether a foldable device is folded or unfolded.

entries.title: PressureRecord
entries.description: The PressureObserver API monitors CPU load, allowing you to adjust workloads in response to available computing resources. Also known as the Compute Pressure API.

entries.title: CookieStoreManager
entries.description: The CookieStore API is an asynchronous and promise-based API for managing cookies. It does not rely on document and so is available to service workers as well.

entries.title: ClipboardItem
entries.description: The navigator.clipboard API asynchronously reads and writes to the system clipboard.

entries.title: PermissionStatus
entries.description: The navigator.permissions API checks whether a permission, such as access to geolocation data, has been granted.

entries.title: WakeLockSentinel
entries.description: The navigator.wakeLock.request("screen") API prevents the device's screen from dimming or being turned off.

entries.title: IdleDeadline (rIC callback)
entries.description: The requestIdleCallback() API queues a function that runs in idle browser time, either at the end of a frame or when the user is inactive. Also known as the background tasks API.

entries.title: ImageBitmap (constructor)
entries.description: The createImageBitmap() global method creates an ImageBitmap object from a source such as an image, SVG, blob, or canvas. An ImageBitmap object represents pixel data that can be drawn to a canvas with lower latency than other types, such as ImageData.

entries.title: Screen Orientation
entries.description: The screen.orientation API gets information about the orientation of the viewport, such as landscape or portrait. With this API, you can adapt an application's layout or behavior in response to changes in orientation.

entries.title: MediaError
entries.description: The  element plays videos or movies, optionally with controls provided by the browser.

entries.title: Audio Session
entries.description: The navigator.audioSession API controls how audio playback interacts with other applications. For example, playing music can pause audio from other applications, while ambient audio can play at the same time.

entries.title: Keyboard API
entries.description: The navigator.keyboard.lock() API allows you to capture keys that are normally reserved by the operating system. It can be used to provide an immersive fullscreen experience such as games.

entries.title: Scheduling API (legacy)
entries.description: The navigator.scheduling.isInputPending() method checks if there are pending input events, such as touch or mouse events. This allows you to yield from long-running scripts that might be blocking user interaction.

entries.title: Navigation API: NavigateEvent
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: NavigationCurrentEntryChangeEvent
entries.description: The navigation API initiates, intercepts, or modifies browser navigation actions. Not to be confused with the navigator API.

entries.title: Cross-document View Transitions: pagereveal
entries.description: View transitions allow you to create animated visual transitions between different states of a document.

entries.title: Cross-document View Transitions: pageswap
entries.description: View transitions allow you to create animated visual transitions between different states of a document.

entries.title: ClipboardEvent
entries.description: The "copy", "cut", and "paste" events fire on elements when the user starts an action with the clipboard.

entries.title: CookieChangeEvent
entries.description: The CookieStore API is an asynchronous and promise-based API for managing cookies. It does not rely on document and so is available to service workers as well.

entries.title: BlobEvent (MediaRecorder data)
entries.description: The MediaRecorder API captures data generated by MediaStream or HTMLMediaElement objects for analysis, processing, or saving to disk. Also known as the Media Recording API.

entries.title: FormDataEvent
entries.description: The  element contains interactive controls for submitting information.

entries.title: MessageEvent
entries.description: The postMessage() global method sends cross-origin messages to windows and workers, including popups and iframes. Also known as cross-document messaging.
